behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Low — this can be reproduced by calling the scoring function with a transaction and config map. REAL-INFRA: None required. MOCK-HIDE: Minimal risk if config is passed directly (no I/O). LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring-model function with table-driven inputs: merchant category thresholds, transaction amount just below/at/above threshold, and expected score contribution. Assert exact numeric score and boundary behavior.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: High — a mocked model can trivially return "fraud" and miss real model weaknesses. REAL-INFRA: Requires real model inference endpoint/runtime. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocking would likely always pass. LLM-DEP: Yes — this is model-quality/classification correctness. MULTI-STEP: Single model decision, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a curated fraud-pattern dataset where rule thresholds are not exceeded. Assert fraud probability crosses the review threshold for known-positive cases and stays below for matched negatives; evaluate pass/fail using acceptance metrics (e.g., minimum recall on this slice).

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Moderate — pure unit tests can miss query/window boundary bugs (timestamp precision, inclusive/exclusive bounds). REAL-INFRA: Local DB/filesystem-backed store is enough; no external API needed. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, repository mocks can hide incorrect filtering logic. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: With a real local datastore, insert timestamped transactions for one user around window edges (inside, exactly on boundary, just outside) plus other users. Execute velocity check through the scoring module stack and assert counted transactions match expected per configured window semantics.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Moderate — unit-testing a boolean expression is easy, but misses real wiring/field-mapping errors between model outputs and decision service. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network requirement beyond local module integration. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, over-mocked model outputs may mask contract mismatch. LLM-DEP: Not for this behavior; only gate logic is under test. MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Run the decision service with real scoring and risk-model adapter interfaces (test doubles only at data source boundaries). Feed a case where scoring result is below threshold and risk-model flag/probability is above threshold. Assert final status is `held_for_review` and audit reason indicates risk-model trigger.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Moderate — unit tests of DTO builders can miss serialization/contract drift when modules are wired. REAL-INFRA: Usually no external dependency needed; local end-to-end module call is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocked payloads can hide missing/renamed fields in real integration. LLM-DEP: No (field presence/shape check). MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the composed fraud-evaluation pipeline and assert response contract includes `risk_score`, `fraud_probability`, and per-model flag/reason fields with correct values/types. Validate against a schema/contract test to catch omissions or naming drift.
